steps.title: Find the slope of line t
steps.content:
The slope $m$ of a line through $(x_1,y_1)$ and $(x_2,y_2)$ is $$m=\frac{y_2-y_1}{x_2-x_1}.$$
Substituting $(0,9)$ and $(1,17)$: $$m=\frac{17-9}{1-0}=\frac{8}{1}=8.$$

steps.title: Write the equation and match the answer
steps.content:
With slope $m=8$ and y‑intercept from point $(0,9)$, the line is $$y=8x+9.$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
